description: Windows Embedded Server — Integrate the Go relay server as an optional embedded subprocess in the Windows WPF client app. Adds ServerProcess (subprocess lifecycle management), ServerConfig (server settings persistence), a server configuration UI section, a server management panel with live logs, and Windows startup integration.

userStories.title: Windows — ServerProcess service
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the WPF app to manage the Go server subprocess so I can host the relay server from my Windows machine.
acceptanceCriteria: New Services/ServerProcess.cs class (INotifyPropertyChanged)
IsRunning: bool property, updated when process starts/exits
LogLines: ObservableCollection<string> (max 500 lines; oldest removed when limit reached)
BinaryPath: string property defaulting to Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "copyeverywhere-server.exe")
ServerConfig reference passed to constructor; used in GetEnvironment()

Start() creates a new Process with RedirectStandardOutput=true, RedirectStandardError=true, UseShellExecute=false, CreateNoWindow=true, sets environment from GetEnvironment(), attaches OutputDataReceived and ErrorDataReceived handlers, sets IsRunning=true
OutputDataReceived/ErrorDataReceived handlers append lines to LogLines via Application.Current.Dispatcher.Invoke (UI-thread safe)
Process Exited event sets IsRunning=false and appends exit code to LogLines
Stop() calls process.Kill(entireProcessTree: true) if process is running, sets IsRunning=false
Restart() calls Stop() then waits 500 ms then calls Start()
Application.Current.Exit event handler calls Stop() for cleanup on app quit
GetEnvironment() returns Dictionary<string,string> from the injected ServerConfig
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows — ServerConfig persistence
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want server settings persisted alongside the client config so everything is in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: New Services/ServerConfig.cs class (INotifyPropertyChanged)

Properties with defaults: Port ("8080"), BindAddress ("0.0.0.0"), StoragePath (Path.Combine(Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData), "CopyEverywhere", "server-data")), TtlHours (24), AuthEnabled (false), AccessToken (""), MaxClipSizeMB (50), ServerEnabled (false), AutoStartServer (false)
UsedSpaceBytes: long property (default 0, populated by RefreshUsedSpaceAsync)
Persisted to Path.Combine(Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData), "CopyEverywhere", "server-config.json")
GetEnvironment() returns Dictionary<string,string> with keys: PORT, BIND_ADDRESS, STORAGE_PATH, TTL_HOURS, AUTH_ENABLED, ACCESS_TOKEN (omitted when AuthEnabled=false or token empty), MAX_CLIP_SIZE_MB
Save() serialises all properties to JSON and writes atomically (write temp file, then File.Replace)
Load() deserialises from JSON; missing file returns defaults without error
RefreshUsedSpaceAsync() calculates total bytes under StoragePath in a background Task, updates UsedSpaceBytes on the UI thread
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows — Server toggle and auto-connect
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the embedded server to start automatically when I enable it, and for the client to connect to it without manual URL entry.
acceptanceCriteria: App.xaml.cs creates ServerConfig and ServerProcess instances and makes them available to MainWindow

On app launch: ServerConfig.Load() called; if AutoStartServer is true, ServerProcess.Start() called; if ServerEnabled is true (or server just started), ConfigStore.HostUrl set to http://localhost:<ServerConfig.Port>
In MainWindow: toggling ServerEnabled CheckBox ON calls ServerProcess.Start() and sets ConfigStore.HostUrl to http://localhost:<ServerConfig.Port> then saves ConfigStore
Toggling ServerEnabled CheckBox OFF calls ServerProcess.Stop(); HostUrl is left unchanged so user can still point at a remote server
If binary is missing (BinaryPath does not exist), toggling ON shows an error message in the server status area and leaves ServerEnabled=false
User can manually edit the Host URL TextBox at any time, overriding the auto-set value
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows — Server configuration UI
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to configure the embedded server from the same settings panel I use for client config.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Embedded Server' GroupBox added to MainWindow XAML below the existing transfer mode section
CheckBox 'Enable embedded server' bound to ServerConfig.ServerEnabled; toggling calls the enable/disable logic from US-085
When ServerEnabled=false, all child controls inside the group are IsEnabled=false (greyed out)
Port TextBox, Bind Address TextBox, TTL (hours) TextBox, Max Clip Size (MB) TextBox — all bound to ServerConfig properties
Storage Path TextBox bound to ServerConfig.StoragePath plus a 'Browse...' Button that opens a FolderBrowserDialog and writes the chosen path back to StoragePath
CheckBox 'Require authentication' bound to ServerConfig.AuthEnabled; when checked, a PasswordBox for Access Token appears (sync via PasswordChanged event)
CheckBox 'Auto-start server on app launch' bound to ServerConfig.AutoStartServer
'Apply & Restart Server' Button: saves ServerConfig, calls ServerProcess.Restart() if running or ServerProcess.Start() if stopped (and ServerEnabled=true)
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows — Server management panel
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want a management panel showing server status, live logs, storage usage, and controls to start/stop/restart.
acceptanceCriteria: Server management section visible only when ServerConfig.ServerEnabled=true; hidden otherwise
Status row: Ellipse (green Fill when IsRunning, red Fill when stopped) + TextBlock ('Running' / 'Stopped')
Listen address TextBlock shows BindAddress:Port from ServerConfig (e.g. '0.0.0.0:8080'), visible when running
Storage used TextBlock shows formatted UsedSpaceBytes (via FormatBytes helper); refreshed by calling RefreshUsedSpaceAsync() when server starts and on a 30-second timer while running
Buttons: 'Start Server' visible when !IsRunning; 'Stop Server' and 'Restart' visible when IsRunning

Log viewer: ScrollViewer containing a TextBlock (FontFamily Consolas or Courier New) bound to LogLines joined by newline; auto-scrolls to bottom on each new log line via ScrollViewer.ScrollToEnd() in a LogLines.CollectionChanged handler
'Clear Logs' Button calls LogLines.Clear()
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows — Run at Windows startup
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the option to launch CopyEverywhere automatically when Windows starts.
acceptanceCriteria: CheckBox 'Run at Windows startup' added to the server configuration UI section (US-086)
Checking the box writes registry value HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run, key name 'CopyEverywhere', value = '"<full path to CopyEverywhere.exe>" --minimized'
Unchecking the box deletes the registry value if it exists
On app launch, App.xaml.cs checks if args contain '--minimized'; if so, MainWindow is not shown (app starts minimized to tray only)
ServerConfig persists the RunAtWindowsStartup bool so the CheckBox state survives restart
SetRunAtStartup(bool) helper on ServerConfig encapsulates the Registry read/write/delete logic
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows — CLAUDE.md conventions update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md updated to document the new embedded server patterns for Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: Windows section in Conventions and gotchas gets new entries for: ServerProcess (subprocess lifecycle, Dispatcher.Invoke for log lines, Kill(entireProcessTree:true), BinaryPath convention), ServerConfig (properties, GetEnvironment() mapping, JSON persistence path), registry startup key pattern, --minimized arg handling in App.xaml.cs
Architecture section updated: Windows client entry notes that ServerProcess and ServerConfig provide optional embedded server support
Source of truth section updated: active iteration points at prd.json and tasks/prd-windows-embedded-server.md
